sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 69

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 69:1
items.html: (to the question) The essential reason for the breaking of the vessels is the two forces that governed as one in the discernment of the Parsa of the second Tzimtzum [restriction] above BYA [Beria, Yetzira, Assiya], which are the Hey Rishona [first Hey] and the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey]. For after the new Light that split the Parsa clothed in the ZAT [seven lower ones] of Nekudim, when it reached the Parsa of the ending it did not discern the Parsa that had been spread there, in the place of Bina of the ten Sefirot of the NHY of AK [Adam Kadmon], by the force of the second Tzimtzum; therefore the seven Kings spread down to the point of the first Tzimtzum, in the place of BYA. But the Hey Tachtona, which had taken her place in the Parsa, awakened at once with her force of Tzimtzum, and then the Lights of the seven Kings immediately departed to their place, and the seven Kings died, as is known. Now, had the force of the Bina of AK not been there in the Parsa, the seven Kings would certainly not have passed the boundary of the Parsa at all, just as they do not pass the boundary of the first Tzimtzum. And had the force of the Hey Tachtona not been in the Parsa, the Lights would not have departed from the seven Kings, and they would have remained alive, like the Partzufim of AK before the second Tzimtzum. Thus the whole cause of the breaking was that the Parsa was in the discernment of a public domain — that is, the discernment of Malchut and Bina governing there in mixture. And this was because the ZAT of Nekudim themselves were in the discernment of a single line, one below another; for the proper joining of the two Heys together existed only in the GAR of Nekudim, who received from its force the discernment of the correction of lines, which joins the two Heys together in the discernment of a single domain (as above in Answer 137, see there). Afterwards this correction was renewed in all the Partzufim of Atzilut, by the ascent of NHY [Netzah, Hod, Yesod] to HGT [Hesed, Gevura, Tiferet], "three within three," whereby the Hey Tachtona was joined to the Hey Rishona in the discernment of a single domain — such that from here on, even when the Partzufim receive the new Light from AB-SAG, which lowers the Hey Tachtona of the second Tzimtzum and returns their AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] to their degree as at first, it will no longer happen that they spread because of this below the Parsa into BYA, for there is no longer any dominion there except of the Hey Tachtona alone. Rather, all the return of the AHP that comes by the force of the new Light of AB is only in the discernment of ascent alone: the former HGT became HBD and the NHY became HGT, and a place was cleared to receive there, in the place of the former NHY, the new NHY, which are the restored AHP. But in no way will the Light spread in the place of these AHP — that is, in BYA — except at the end of correction; then "the feet will reach the feet," and the feet of Atzilut will spread down to BYA, and not before. (p. 765, beginning "And you find")
